TestCase z39Change(TestCase t1) {
    TestCase follow_case = t1;
    // --- Memory handling code generated based on the rule ---
    if (t1.n > 0 && t1.b != NULL) {
        int* follow_case_b = (int*)malloc(t1.n * sizeof(int));
        memcpy(follow_case_b, t1.b, t1.n * sizeof(int));
        follow_case.b = follow_case_b;
    }
    // ------------------------------------
    
    // Defensive code generated based on rule (a) to prevent errors when n < 1 or b is NULL
    if (follow_case.n < 1 || follow_case.b == NULL) {
        return follow_case;
    }
    
    // Specific operation code generated based on the rule
    // Find all indices where element < 999 inside follow_case.b
    int count = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < follow_case.n; i++) {
        if (follow_case.b[i] < 999) {
            count++;
        }
    }
    if (count == 0) {
        // No element less than 999, do nothing
        return follow_case;
    }
    // Pick a random index among those less than 999
    int* valid_indices = (int*)malloc(count * sizeof(int));
    int idx = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < follow_case.n; i++) {
        if (follow_case.b[i] < 999) {
            valid_indices[idx++] = i;
        }
    }
    // Use a basic pseudo-random selection based on current time to avoid dependency on rand()
    // Note: time() requires #include <ctime> but we skip includes as per instructions; assume rand() is safe here
    int rand_index = valid_indices[rand() % count];
    free(valid_indices);
    follow_case.b[rand_index] = 999;
    
    return follow_case;
}
